given: Private publication metadata and deterministic self-contained page content
when: Private previews, public page rendering, and conversation fragments are produced
acceptance: Rendered pages do not expose internal system modules
Published and private content have distinct routes
Internal Page and thumbnail URLs remain relative to the current desktop or mobile origin
pa-cli pages publish returns the managed-tunnel HTTPS URL alongside the canonical internalUrl, or an explicit unavailable-domain notice when no accessible domain exists
Public shareUrl is separate and never replaces the managed delivery URL or internal URL

Every new Page persists different desktop and mobile thumbnails and each client renders its matching thumbnail; when omitted, the CLI generates distinct device gallery previews without browser capture

The Agent lists and inspects registered Page templates before generation, selects every semantic match, and passes the template contract, linked Skill, implementation version, exact generator, artifact marker and user-owned visual acceptance contract into a child task
Page publication returns a stable pageId and Page Activity must target that id so the Activity card opens Page detail
Page Activity uses the stored mobile thumbnail as representative feed media without scraping Page HTML

The authenticated Pages module exposes a compact static template catalog whose versioned cover can be cached, whose artwork matches the detail SU style, whose detail shell appears before deferred WebGL initialization, and whose Web preview fills the demonstration stage
A template detail opens its full Page example in a separate authenticated tab

The built-in renovation delivery template is linked to interior-design, is generated by the registered implementation with a verifiable marker and version, keeps SU design, the user-supplied redacted floor plan, annotated revisions and the Agent requirement digest in one delivery, locates demolition by plan adjacency, uses architectural dimension lines for measurements, keeps label visibility available in mobile landscape, supports desktop and mobile landscape touch layouts, performs no Agent browser self-acceptance, and leaves visual and interaction acceptance to the user
